i am glad to see the new york times is a moutpiece for the kamala campaign but the facts are 

ongoing high migration rates despite claims of progress us border crossings reached record highs in 2023 while there may have been temporary declines following specific policies the overall trend under this administration has been increased migration numbers compared to previous years critics argue that isolated decreases arent reflective of a sustained comprehensive border control strategy especially with fluctuating spikes in migration

incomplete diplomatic solutions the narrative suggests that diplomatic agreements with countries like guatemala and mexico have effectively controlled migration however many of these agreements lack enforcement mechanisms that reliably prevent migrant flows to the us migration routes are dynamic with migrants frequently adapting to new barriers the current strategy may slow migration but does not prevent it effectively as migrants continue to circumvent local barriers

asylum system bottlenecks and overload the current administrations restrictions on asylum may appear stringent but theyve arguably worsened the asylum systems backlog without meaningful reform migrants often attempt crossing illegally as asylumseeking avenues become blocked overwhelmed or increasingly difficult to navigate instead of securing the border these barriers may simply increase unauthorized entries placing additional strain on the already stretched resources at entry points

deterrence messaging issues the biden administrations mixed messaging on immigration may inadvertently encourage migration the administrations public stance on supporting refugees and humane treatment can appear contradictory to strict enforcement efforts leading to confusion and mixed interpretations among migrants some of whom may still see the us as open due to policy rollbacks on deportations and removals

reliance on shortterm fixes rather than tackling root causes directly critics argue that the administration relies on temporary solutions like increasing foreign troops at the border or placing shortlived restrictions on asylum these fixes lack sustainability and risk unraveling under future administrations suggesting an absence of longterm bipartisan solutions
